text:
LA County Public Health - Maternal, Child, & Adolescent Health
CPSP Resources
Please explore these resources put together by the
LA County CPSP
Team to support pregnant and postpartum individuals who are being serviced by CPSP providers. This page brings together key guidance, handouts, and practical tools across CPSP service areas—including General CPSP resources, Health Education, Nutrition, Psychosocial support, and Breast/Chestfeeding—to help teams deliver patient-centered education, counseling, screening, referrals, and ongoing client support.
Resources are organized by topic for easy use in clinical care, coordination, and referrals. This is not an exhaustive list and may be updated over time. If you experience access issues (such as broken links or download errors), please report them using the
contact section below
.
General CPSP Resources
This section includes core CPSP program resources that support service
									implementation, compliance, and high-quality care delivery across the perinatal
									period.
CPSP Provider Handbook:
The
Comprehensive Perinatal Services Program (CPS) Provider
												Handbook
is designed to help a CPSP provider with the
											information and tools needed to implement the program at their site.
Steps to Take Guidelines:
The purpose of these guidelines is to provide CPSP staff members with the
											information they need to effectively assess situations, provide
											interventions, and refer appropriately.
Steps to Take (Reproducible Handout):
Printable client education handouts that support CPSP interventions and are
											available in
English and Spanish
.
Title 22 CPSP Regulations:
The official state regulations that define CPSP program standards,
											requirements, and allowable services.
Health Education
This section features health education resources that support counseling,
									prevention, and informed decision-making for pregnant and postpartum individuals
									across a range of perinatal health topics.

Nutrition
This section includes nutrition resources to support counseling, education, and
									referrals related to healthy eating, weight management, food access, and
									nutrition-related conditions during pregnancy and the postpartum period.

10

Psychosocial
This section includes psychosocial resources to support screening, counseling,
									crisis response, and referrals related to safety, mental health, and emotional
									well-being during pregnancy and the postpartum period.
View:
5
10
15
20
First
Prev
Next
Last
The following are worksheets designed to facilitate seamless communication, enhance
									the quality of care, and support positive outcomes for both providers and patients
									in the perinatal period.
Cognitive Behavioral Strategies for Mothers:
This
CBT worksheet
is designed to support new mothers
												in building resilience, enhancing emotional well-being, and navigating
												the joys and challenges of motherhood with confidence.
Tips for Anxiety & Stress:
This resource helps birthing parents
identify, understand, and
													manage common stressors and anxieties
associated with
												caring for a newborn.
Tips for Managing Sleep:
This worksheet provides
practical strategies to support rest and
													maintain well-being
during the postpartum period.
Breast / Chestfeeding
This section includes breast/chestfeeding resources that support lactation
									education, peer and community support, and referrals for pregnant and postpartum
									individuals.
